title: TTCL Sign-Event spec — canonical Sign→event bridge
description: The single canonical definition of TTCL Sign observation event types: their wire-level JSON Schemas (payload shape) and the Sign factory inputs that produce conforming payloads. The first Sign→event bridge — ttcl.observation.emitted — is declared here. Every TS runtime derives from this file via codegen (gen-ttcl-artifacts.mjs); drift is caught by scripts/check-ttcl-artifacts-drift.mjs (regenerates + string-diffs the 3 generated artifacts) and monad-ecosystem/tests/integration/schema-validators.test.ts (positive + negative cases prove the validators check something).

ttcl.observation.emitted.description: A TTCL Sign observation emitted on the signal bus — the first Sign→event bridge. The payload is a Sign (the core TTCL semiotic unit: modality + domain + pps + peirce classification + optional trace + optional triadic ancestry). The bus envelope (SignalEvent) is validated separately by shared/schemas/signal-event.json; this schema validates the payload only. Emitted by the TTCL runtime when a Sign is materialized for downstream gnosis/dove/hepar consumption.

schema.title: TTCLObservationEvent payload
schema.description: Payload of a ttcl.observation.emitted SignalEvent — a TTCL Sign observation. Matches Sign<M,T> at monad-ecosystem/packages/ttcl/src/types.ts:48 and PeirceSignature at monad-ecosystem/packages/sovereign-types/src/peirce/models.ts:25.

modality.description: Position on the modal lattice: HYBRID ⊤ → ICON / INDEX / SYMBOL → PURE ⊥.

domain.description: The TTCL domain this sign carries.

pps.description: Primary Parameter Schema sync score — a per-emission runtime position on the manifold ring.

peirce.description: PeirceSignature — the 66-class manifold classification. The manifold (shared/peirce-spec/peirce_sign_classes.json, surfaced in @sovereign/types) is the sole source of truth for label/path/weights; sign_class_id must be a valid manifold id in [0, 65].

mode.description: Coarse mode derived from the sign class path.

sign_class_id.description: Manifold class id. Unknown ids throw UnknownSignClassError at runtime.

sign_class_label.description: Human-readable class label from the manifold.

trace.description: Intention traceability metadata. Required by the bus for governance-relevant, action-bearing, or economically meaningful event types. See docs/CHARTER.md §4.

intentionId.description: Unique identifier for the intention chain that produced this event.

source.description: Agent, service, or contributor that originated the intention.

parentEventId.description: Parent event in the intention chain, if any.

constraintEnvelopeId.description: Constraint envelope governing the action that produced this event.

narrativePurposeId.description: Narrative purpose tag linking the event to a declared objective.

createdAt.description: ISO-8601 timestamp when the trace record was created.

domains.description: Triadic ancestry — domains this sign represents. A raw single-domain sign carries [domain]; a HYBRID from compose(theo, tech, cosmo) carries all three. Consumed by the constitution scorer C1.

noRlhf.description: Caller-set flag (default true) that no RLHF signal is present. Consumed by the constitution scorer C5.

triadicObservation.description: The well-formed triadic HYBRID observation: compose(Theology, Technology, Cosmology) dominant peirce = class 8 (Delome-Symbol-Legisign, FORMAL_THOUGHT, thirdness 0.85 — the most argumentative class). Spans all three domains; would pass the constitution triadic gate. pps 0.30 places it in the volatile band (genuine sync tension, not a locked state).

rawTechnologyPacket.description: A raw single-domain TECHNOLOGY index — class 2 (Dicent-Indexical-Sinsign, EXPERIENCE, secondness 1.0). Single-domain by design, so it scores < 0.72 on the constitution C1 (tripartite) criterion and is structurally incapable of passing the Triadic Minimal Gate alone. pps 1.00 = fully synced raw packet. Used as the negative-shape counterpoint to triadicObservation.

ttcl.observation.emitted.rationale: TTCL is side-effect-free today (ttcl/src/types.ts:38); the bus never references Sign. This event type is the first bridge: a Sign materialized as a bus event so downstream gnosis/dove/hepar consumers can observe semiotic state without coupling to the TTCL runtime. The payload schema is the wire contract; the factory inputs are the codegen source for makeSign(...) wrappers that produce manifold-consistent, schema-conforming payloads (the manifold stays the sole source of truth for the peirce block — factories call makeSign → getManifold().get(classId), never hand-assemble PeirceSignature).
